title: ICC in POSH cases can't hide name of instigator of false sexual harassment complaint: Bombay High Court
description: Internal complaints committees (ICCs) cannot shield an identified instigator of a false sexual harassment complaint by anonymising them as an “unknown source” in their report, the Bombay High Court at Goa has held.Justice Neela Gokhale ruled that once the ICC chose to rely on the complainant’s retraction letter, it could not selectively omit the name of the person she had clearly identified as the instigator. Justice Neela GokhaleThe Court remarked that treating that person as merely an “un
